# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd dolt pull            (pull beads updates from main)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."     (commit code changes)
```

**Note:** This is an ephemeral branch (no upstream). Code is merged to main locally, not pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: run `bd dolt pull` at session start
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads updates from Dolt remote
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
bd dolt pull                # Pull latest beads from main
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit your changes
# Merge to main when ready (local merge, not push)
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

## Persistent Memories (5)

Stored via `bd remember`. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`. Remove with `bd forget <key>`.

### issue-102-fully-resolved-with-4-distinct-fixes
Issue #102 fully resolved with 4 distinct fixes (see commits on worktree-102 branch): Bug A=disable stale-tool timer for Claude Code in core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go; Bug B=new LastWasUserInterrupt field in domain metrics, set only on the '[Request interrupted by user' text marker, used by state_classifier cancellation rule instead of LastToolResultWasError; Bug C=removed toolUseCount/toolResultCount counters in pkg/tailer/tailer.go, HasOpenToolCall now derived from len(lastOpenToolNames) — single source of truth eliminates counter desync on orphan tool_result events from --continue/compact; Bug D=allow-list of terminal stop_reasons {end_turn,stop_sequence,refusal,tool_use} in claudecode/parser.go, everything else (including max_tokens, pause_turn) becomes assistant_streaming. Total flicker reduction across 4 fixtures: 7368 → 1 (the 1 residual is a legitimate 5.8s turn_done-triggered ready episode, not a bug). Replay harness lives at core/cmd/replay-session with scripts in scripts/replay-fixtures.sh and scripts/find-flicker-sessions.sh, fixtures under testdata/issue-102/fixtures.

### issue-102-has-two-distinct-flicker-root-causes
Issue #102 has TWO distinct flicker root causes (replay of current Claude Code session captures both): (1) 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout fires during long Bash runs (working→waiting→working), as previously documented. (2) ClassifyState misinterprets benign tool failures as ESC cancellation: in core/application/services/state_classifier.go the rule '(state==working|waiting) && !HasOpenToolCall && LastEventType==user && LastToolResultWasError → ready (cancellation)' fires whenever any tool returns is_error=true (grep with no matches, failed Bash, etc.), bouncing the session to ready then immediately back to working on the next event. The fix needs to distinguish actual ESC interrupts (user message text starts with '[Request interrupted by user') from normal tool failures. The current claudecode/parser.go at line 84 already detects the ESC text marker — the cancellation rule should check that signal, not just LastToolResultWasError.

### issue-102-root-cause-is-the-15s-defaultstaletooltimeout
Issue #102 root cause is the 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout in core/application/services/session_detector.go, NOT the MessageHistory sliding window. The replay tool (core/cmd/replay-session) shows the pattern clearly: any Bash tool that runs >15s repeatedly trips the stale-tool timer → waiting → tool result arrives → working. On a 558h fixture it produced 82 flickers / 42 stale-timer fires, all on Bash. Fix should bump the timeout for Bash specifically or use a more conservative anchor-event approach. Replay reproductions live in testdata/issue-102/reports/.

### issue-114-stuck-working-turn-done-fix
GH #114 resolved via narrow safety-net in core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go: when parsed.EventType==turn_done, reset lastOpenToolNames=nil. Root cause: FIFO could hold phantom entries from orphan tool_result (--continue/compact), multi-line assistant splits, or parallel tool_use drift — HasOpenToolCall=len(FIFO)>0 gates IsAgentDone() via short-circuit at core/domain/session/session.go:120 before it can check LastEventType==turn_done, pinning sessions at working. Reset runs before addMessageEvent() sets LastEventType, so cleared FIFO and updated event type land on the same computeMetrics snapshot. Unit test: TestHasOpenToolCall_TurnDoneReconciles in core/pkg/tailer/tool_call_test.go. Replay fixtures: testdata/replay/claudecode/08-issue-114-stuck-working-a.jsonl, 09-issue-114-stuck-working-b.jsonl. Follow-up: structural id-based tool tracking rewrite (map[tool_use_id]name instead of FIFO) — file as own issue.

### pr-106-bundles-two-flicker-fixes-1-97
PR #106 bundles two flicker fixes: (1) #97 stale-tool timer + StatePolicy abstraction removed (19 files +46/-806); (2) Layer 1 from #102 — Claude Code parser splits [Request interrupted by user] (ESC, sets IsUserInterrupt) from [Request interrupted by user for tool use] (denial, sets new IsToolDenial). Cancellation rule only fires on ESC now, suppressing working→ready→working flicker on every denial. LastWasToolDenial propagates through tailer + domain.SessionMetrics. Layer 2 tracked as GH #108 — uses PermissionRequest hook (NOT Notification), reuses irrlichd HTTP server for ingestion via POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode, claudecode-adapter-isolated, exposes single generic PermissionPending boolean on shared metrics (avoids reintroducing the deleted StatePolicy abstraction). Key correction: bypassPermissions is NOT a sufficient gate predictor — protected paths (.git/.claude/.vscode/.idea/.husky) still prompt.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.claude/skills/ir:test-sub-fg

# Test Foreground Subagent Detection

Launch foreground agents in 2 waves to verify irrlicht detects and displays them correctly.
Run both waves back-to-back without pausing for user confirmation. Do NOT run any checker process, curl commands, or daemon API queries between waves — just launch the agents and print status messages.

## Wave 1: 3 Foreground Subagents

Launch 3 Agent tool calls in a single message (parallel). Each agent should do a small read-only task that takes ~10 seconds:

1. **Agent 1** (subagent_type: Explore): "Count the number of Go files in the core/ directory. Report the total."
2. **Agent 2** (subagent_type: Explore): "Find all test files in core/e2e/ and list their names. Report the list."
3. **Agent 3** (subagent_type: Explore): "Search for all exported functions in core/domain/session/session.go. Report their names."

After all 3 complete, print "Wave 1 done (3 foreground subagents)." Then immediately proceed to Wave 2.

## Wave 2: 3 More Foreground Subagents

Launch 3 Agent tool calls in a single message (parallel):

1. (subagent_type: Explore): "Find all imports of the 'sync' package across core/. Report the file list."
2. (subagent_type: Explore): "Count the total lines of Go code in core/pkg/tailer/. Report the count."
3. (subagent_type: Explore): "List all interface definitions in core/ports/. Report their names and methods."

After all 3 complete, print "All waves complete (6 foreground subagents). Check the UI for the full picture."

## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

## Search Results: Error Handling Patterns in core/application/

### Count Summary
**Total: 0 instances of fmt.Errorf found**

The codebase in `core/application/` does not use `fmt.Errorf` for error creation.

### Error Handling Patterns Actually Used

**1. Silent Error Ignoring (Most Common)**
Files primarily use the pattern `if err != nil { return }` or `_ = repo.Save(state)` to silently ignore errors. Examples:
- `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/application/services/pid_manager.go:79-84` - Loads session state with error checked but silently returned if not found

### Architecture Insight
The codebase follows a **fail-silent pattern** where:
- Repository operations (`Save`, `Load`, `Delete`) can fail but errors don't propagate
- Failures are logged through a centralized `Logger` interface
- The application continues operation even when individual operations fail (resilience-focused design)
- No custom error types or error wrapping - all error handling is implicit</result>
